awesomeList.descriptionHTML: A curated list of awesome Common Lisp frameworks, libraries and other shiny stuff.
awesomeList.description: A curated list of awesome Common Lisp frameworks, libraries and other shiny stuff.

nodes.itemHTML: • #lisp on Libera Chat - IRC channel for all Lisp dialects.
• #clschool on Libera Chat - IRC channel for learning Common Lisp.
• #lispcafe on Libera Chat - IRC channel for off-topic discussions.
• #commonlisp on Libera Chat - main Common Lisp IRC channel.
• quicklisp-systemsstars19 - Search, browse and load Quicklisp systems from Emacs.
• sigilstars0 - A Parenscript to
• CLPM - A package manager for Common Lisp that strives to cleanly separate the package manager process itself from the client image that uses it. BSD_2Clause.
• Qlotstars242 - A project-local library installer, similar to Bundler or Carton. Expat.
• Quicksys - install systems from multiple Quicklisp distributions. MIT.
• Portalstars10 - Portable websockets for Common Lisp using usocket. LLGPL.
• crypto-shortcutsstars19 - Collection of common crypto shortcuts. zlib.
• ubiquitousstars20 - A library providing easy-to-use persistent configuration storage. zlib.
• 👍 Qtoolsstars168 - A Qt toolkit, based on CommonQt. zlib Also Qtools-uistars29 (premade UI components), with videos.

• dissect - when a lot of projects use the “trivial-backtrace” system that just gives them a string with a backtrace, Dissect allows you to capture, step, and completely inspect the stack trace on a variety of Lisp implementations. Also very useful for logging and other situations where execution is automatically continued, but the information of the current stack is still useful to store somewhere. zlib.
• trivial-argumentsstars16 - A portable library to retrieve the arguments list of a function. zlib.

• definitionsstars10 - a general definitions introspection library. It gives you the ability to retrieve definitions or bindings associated with designators such as symbols, packages, and names in general. zlib.
• for - A concise, lispy and extensible iteration macro. Unlike loop it is extensible and sensible, and unlike iterate it does not require code-walking and is easier to extend. zlib.
• 👍 Plumpstars87 - A lenient HTML/XML parser, tolerant on malformed markup. zlib. Best used with lquerystars65 and clssstars24.
• clip - An HTML template processor where the templates are written in HTML. zlib.
• deploy - A toolkit for binary deployment of Lisp applications, with extra support for foreign shared libraries. zlib.

• 3d-matrices - A library implementing common matrix calculations, with an emphasis on 2x2,3x3, and 4x4 matrices as commonly used in graphics. It provides some numerical functions as well, but those are not the focus. The library is heavily optimised, so it is not made of pretty code. zlib.
• simple-tasksstars12 - A very simple task scheduling framework. zlib.
• deedsstars18 - Deeds is an Extensible Event Delivery System. It allows for efficient event delivery to multiple handlers with a complex event filtering system. zlib.
• cl-allstars11 - A script to run Lisp snippets in multiple implementations. This allows you to quickly compare implementation behaviour and differences. zlib.
• Parachutestars32 - An extensible and cross-compatible testing framework. With test dependencies, conditions, fixtures and restarts. zlib.
• ratifystars21 - A collection of utilities to ratify, validate and parse inputs. zlib.
• Staplestars45 - a tool to generate documentation pages using an HTML template. Uses the existing README, adds docstrings, crossreferences and links to the CLHS. zlib.
• pathname-utilsstars5 - A collection of utilities to help with pathname operations. zlib.
• legit - an interface to the Git binary. zlib.
• oxenfurtstars12 - A client library for the Oxford dictionary API. zlib.
• language-codes - A database library for ISO language codes. zlib
• system-locale - A library to retrieve the user's preferred language, so that your application may choose a sensible default. zlib.
• multilang-documentation - Allows writing docstrings in multiple languages, for truly internationally documented libraries. zlib.
• verbose - A fast and highly configurable logging framework. zlib.
• Trialstars190 - Trial is an OpenGL game engine with a heavy focus on modularity. It is supposed to provide a large toolkit of useful bits and pieces from which you can create a game. zlib.
• cl-mpg123 and cl-out123, bindings libraries for libmpg123 and libout123 respectively, giving you fast and easy to use mp3 decoding and cross-platform audio output. zlib.
• trivial-gamekit – with this small framework you would be able to make simple 2D games: draw basic geometric forms, images and text, play sounds and listen to mouse and keyboard input. MIT.
• cl-gamepad - Access to gamepads and joysticks on Windows, Mac OS, and Linux. zlib.
• viralitystars63 - A component-based game engine written in Common Lisp MIT.

• Lishstars17 - lish may someday be a lisp shell. GPL3.
    • supports tab-completion of executables in the path and Lisp symbols, allows to write and to mix shell commands and Lisp code, has a tiny REPL and an interactive debugger, and more.
• cl-cxx-jitstars18 - Common Lisp and C++ interoperation with JIT. MIT.
• cl-jsonstars0 - A highly customizable JSON encoder and decoder. MIT.
• Snoozestars140 - A RESTful web framework. Web server agnostic. Currently has support for Hunchentoot and Clack. Routes are just functions and HTTP conditions are just Lisp conditions. LLGPL.

• lisp-stat - an environment for statistical computing, conceptually similar to R, that is also suitable for front-line production deployments. "It grew out of a desire to have an environment for rapidly prototyping analytical and A.I. solutions, and move directly to production environments with minimal friction."
    • https://lisp-stat.dev/
    • ships Luke Tierney's XLisp-Stat (a predecessor of R) as well as newer libraries.

• 👍 cl-gserverstars65 - an Erlang inspired GenServer. It is meant to encapsulate state, but also to execute async operations. Also with actors. Functionality regarding state is not unsimilar to Clojure's Agent or cl-actors. MIT.
• doplusstars2 – another extensible iteration library, similar to :for.

• Consfiguratorstars10 - Lisp declarative configuration management system. You can use it to configure hosts as root, deploy services as unprivileged users, build and deploy containers, and produce disc images. GPL3.
• rollbar.lispstars2 - interface to Rollbar.com, an error tracking software.

• CLOGstars200 - The Common Lisp Omnificent GUI. Uses web technology to produce graphical user interfaces for applications locally or remotely. BSD_3Clause.
    • CLOG is based on the ideas of GNOGA, a framework the author wrote for Ada and used in commercial production code since 2013.

• Interactive SSR - ISSR allows you to make interactive web pages without writing client scripting. No knowledge about Javascript or DOM is necessary.
    • it is not unlike Phoenix LiveView or Hotwire.
• Weblocksstars76 - A widgets-based framework with a built-in ajax update mechanism that "solves the JavaScript problem". LLGPL.

• CXML - XML parser and serializer, with a range of extension libraries. LLGPL.
    • 👍 has an incremental parser, allowing to parse big files.

• Emacs4CLstars153 - A tiny Emacs initialization file to quickly set up vanilla Emacs for Common Lisp programming. Comes with a line-by-line explanation of every line of code in the initialization file.
• cl-docker-images - Docker images for ABCL, CCL, ECL, and SBCL on Windows (amd64) and Alpine and Debian (amd64, arm64, arm/v7) BSD_2Clause.

• cmdstars31 - utility for running external programs. Protects against shell interpolation, built with multi-threaded programs in mind, Windows support. MIT.
    • uiop:run-program (synchronous) and uiop:launch-program (async) are shipped with ASDF and available on all modern implementations. See the CL Cookbook: running external programs.
• Lisp for the Web - A book that walks you through building a voting web application in three tiers.

• Section on Web Development in The Common Lisp Cookbook - An introductory tutorial covering web server setup, routing, weblocks, templating, error handling, packaging, hot reloading, database connection, and deployment, amongst other topics in the current lisp web development ecosystem.
• Vellumstars30 - Data Frames for Common Lisp. BSD_2Clause.

• rtg-mathstars44 - a selection of the math routines most commonly needed for making realtime graphics in lisp (2, 3 and 4 component vectors, 3x3 and 4x4 matrices, quaternions, spherical and polar coordinates). BSD_2Clause.
• magiclstars143 - Matrix Algebra proGrams In Common Lisp based on BLAS/LAPACK and Expokit, by Rigetti Computing. BSD_3Clause.
• lisp-matrixstars60 - A matrix package. FreeBSD.
• common-lisp-statstars160 - Common Lisp statistics library. FreeBSD.
• cmu-infixstars20 - A library for writing infix mathematical notation in Common Lisp. See also polisherstars3.
• flatpack-common-lisp - A BuildStream project for building Flatpak based runtime environments for Common Lisp applications.

• ⭐ Parenscript - A translator from Common Lisp to Javascript. 3-clause BSD. See Trident-modestars62, an Emacs mode that provides live interaction with the browser.unlicence.
    • paren6stars13 - a set of ES6 macros for Parenscript.

• calispelstars19 - CSP-like channels for common lisp. With blocking, optionally buffered channels and a "CSP select" statement. ISC-style.
    • "It is complete, flexible and easy to use. I would recommend Calispel over Lparallel and ChanL." @Ambrevar. discussion
• ⭐ local-time - A development library for manipulating date and time information in a semi-standard manner. 3-clause BSD.
• ADW-Charting - A simple chart drawing library written completely in Common Lisp. Also includes a backend to Google's chart service. BSD-like.
• bststars6 - Binary Search Tree. GPL3.
• commonlisp-vscode - an extension to support syntax highlight, auto completion, documentation on hover, go to definition, compile & load file, REPL. It is On GitHubstars29.
• strict-paredit-vscode - structural editing and navigation like Emacs.
• ABCLJstars70 - dead easy Clojure to Common lisp interop. EPL-2.0.

• Building Problem Solvers (PDF) by Ken Forbus and Johan de Kleer, made available for free by MIT Press - a unique book among standard artificial intelligence texts in combining science and engineering, theory and craft to describe the construction of AI reasoning systems, and including code illustrating the ideas.
• clmlstars224 - originally developed by Mathematicl Systems Inc., a Japanese company. With a tutorial. LLGPL.
• gtwiwtgstars33 - A lazy sequences library. Similar to 'series' but not as complete. However it has a 'modern' API with stuff like take, filter, for, fold, etc. that is easy to use.
• physical-quantitiesstars26 - a library that provides a numeric type with optional unit and/or uncertainty for computations with automatic error propagation. GPL2
• parseqstars13 - a library for parsing sequences such as strings and lists using parsing expression grammars. Inspired by Esrap. GPL2.
• zippystars5 - A ZIP archive format library based on 3bz. zlib.
• 👍 FSet - A functional, set-theoretic collections data structure library. LLGPL.
• hu.dwim.walker - a code walker and unwalker (aka AST parser and unparser). BSD. See also this blog post.
• f-underscore - a tiny library of functional programming utils. (f_ (+ _ _)) -> (lambda (_) (+ _ _)). Public domain.
• 👍 Dexadorstars221 - An HTTP client, that aims at replacing Drakma. MIT.
• cl-cronstars3 - A simple tool that provides cron like facilities. GPL3.
• ⭐ uiop and its pathname package (replaces cl-fad). uiop is part of ASDF3 and as thus is shipped in many implementations. MIT.
• cl-pslib - a (thin) wrapper around the pslib library for generating PostScript files. Also cl-pslib-barcode. LLGPL.
• xmlsstars6 - A small, simple, non-validating XML parser. 3-clause BSD.
• cl-cudastars185 - A library to use NVIDIA CUDA in Common Lisp programs. LLGPL.
• ⭐ esrapstars43 - Packrat parser. Expat.
• texpstars4 - A DSL to generate TeX. AGPL-3.0.
• chipzstars14 - A decompression library. 3-clause BSD.
• Salza2 - A library for creating compressed data. FreeBSD.
• LispWork's CAPI - A portable GUI toolkit, with mobile runtime. Proprietary, but comes with a free version.
• flutestars46 - An easily composable HTML5 generation library with the most simplistic syntax. MIT.
• lsxstars61 and markupstars27 - Two JSX-like templating engines, where HTML tags are Common Lisp code. markup comes with an Emacs package.
• STMXstars204 - High performance Transactional Memory for Common Lisp. LLGPL.
• sbclistars46 - a readline REPL for SBCL. With completion, quick commands, optional syntax highlighting (with pygments), and no interactive debugger. GPL3.
• cl-liballegrostars35 - Interface and bindings to the Allegro 5 game programming library. zlib.
• with-c-syntaxstars101 - a fun package which introduces the C language syntax into Common Lisp. (Yes, this package is not for practical coding, I think.) WTFPL Licence.
• cl-advice - an attempt of portable layer advice library for SBCL, CCL, LispWorks and Allegro. Not in Quicklisp.
• 👍Clackstars878 - A web application environment inspired by Rack and WSGI. LLGPL. Provides a unified interface to a webserver of choice (default is Hunchentoot). With more getting started guide.
• trivial-dostars7 - Additional dolist style macros for Common Lisp. MIT.
• piclstars11 - An (almost) complete port of Python's itertools package, complete with laziness where applicable, and not relying on cl-cont. MIT.
• cl-mangostars1 - A minimalist CouchDB 2.x database client. BSD_3Clause. See also clouchdb - Library for interacting with CouchDB. FreeBSD.

• 👍 easy-routesstars27 - a routes handling system on top of Hunchentoot. It supports dispatch based on HTTP method, arguments extraction from the url path, decorators, url generation from route name, etc. MIT.
• sblintstars89 - a linter for Common Lisp source code using SBCL, suited for Reviewdog (slides). BSD_2Clause.
• trivial-formatterstars13 - code formatter for Common Lisp. MIT.
• Temperancestars43 - logic programming. MIT. A focus on performance, with General Game Playing in mind.
• Aprilstars249 - The APL programming language (a subset thereof) compiling to Common Lisp. Replace hundreds of lines of number-crunching code with a single line of APL. Apache2.
• snakesstars35 - Python style generators for Common Lisp. Includes a port of itertools. Apache2.
• sparserstars35 - A natural language understanding system for English. Eclipse.
• one-more-re-nightmarestars26 - a fast-ish regular expression compiler in Common Lisp. BSD_2Clause.
• Cleshstars49 - extends Common Lisp to embed shell code in a manner similar to perl's backtick. FreeBSD.
• cl-yaccstars45 - a LALR(1) parser generator. MIT.
• cl-shlexstars6 - simple lexical analyzer for shell-like syntaxes. MIT.
• smugstars88 - parser combinators for Common Lisp. SMUG makes it simple to create quick extensible recursive descent parsers without funky syntax or impenetrable macrology. MIT.
• gettextstars5 - a port of the gettext runtime to Common Lisp. GPL3.
• json-schemastars5 - A library for validating data against schemas of drafts 4, 6, 7, and 2019-09 of the JSON Schema standard. LLGPL.
• burgled-batteriesstars98 - A bridge between Python and Common Lisp. The goal is that Lisp programs can use Python libraries. Not available on Quicklisp. MIT.
• ⭐ cl-interpolstars38 - A set of reader modifications to allow string interpolation. BSD.
• mailgunstars9 - A thin wrapper to post HTML emails through mailgun.com. unlicence.
• cubestars28 - Kubernetes client library for Common LISP generated from the Swagger specification. MIT.
• Moirastars24 - Monitor and restart background threads. In-lisp process supervisor. MIT.
• clachestars2 - General caching facility. LLGPL.
• factsstars32 - an in-memory graph database with transactions and rollbacks, logging/replay and dumping/loading to/from disk. BSD-style license (ISC).
• ecrepl - an interactive REPL for the C language. BSD_2Clause.
• ⭐ iterate - An iteration construct for Common Lisp which is extensible and Lispier. MIT.
• cl-ssh-keysstars6 - Common Lisp system for generating and parsing of OpenSSH keys. BSD_3Clause.

• cl-bcryptstars8 - Common Lisp system for parsing and generating bcrypt password hashes. BSD_3Clause.
    • see also cl-passstars23, using pbkdf2.
• cl-json-pointerstars5 - A JSON Pointer implementation. MIT.

• cl-agraphstars18, a minimal client for AllegroGraph. AllegroGraph is a horizontally distributed, multi-model (document and graph), entity-event knowledge graph technology. It is proprietary and has a free version with a limit of 5 million triples.
• cl-neo4jstars22 - neo4j RESTFUL client interface.

• Sublime Text (proprietary) has Common Lisp support with its SublimeREPL and Slyblimestars25 packages. Slyblime is an implementation of SLY and it uses the same backend (SLYNK). It ships advanced features including a debugger with stack frame inspection.
• /r/learnlisp - a subreddit to ask questions and get help about Lisp
• hunchentoot-errorsstars4 - Augments Hunchentoot error pages and logs with request and session information. MIT.
• linux-packaging - build .deb, .rpm or .pkg packages for your application with a single ASDF declaration. Uses fpm under the hood. MIT.
• cl-brewerstars9 - Homebrew formula builder for (command line) common lisp applications. Public domain.
• 👍 cl-formsstars20 - Web forms handling library for Common lisp. MIT.
• hunchentoot-authstars1 - implementing user authentication with passwords. BSD.
• kaistars72 - A high-level plotter library for Common Lisp. A wrapper around the Plotly JS library. MIT.
• cl-sparkstars82 - sparkline strings for the console: (spark '(1 1 2 3 5 8)) => "▁▁▂▃▅▇". MIT.

• vivace-graphstars106 - graph database & Prolog implementation. Takes design inspiration from CouchDB, neo4j and AllegroGraph. It implements an ACID-compliant object graph model with user-defined indexes and map-reduce views. It also implements a master / slave replication scheme for redundancy and horizontal read scaling. Querying the graph is accomplished via a number of Lisp methods or via a Prolog-like query language. MIT.
• Lisp Web Tales - Simple tutorials using hunchentoot, cl-redisstars156, restasstars245, SEXMLstars21, Postmodernstars276, and more.
• Geany-lispstars6 is an experimental lisp mode for the Geany editor.
• Lisp Tips - A blog with useful tips and tricks. A continuation of Common Lisp tips.
• Lisp project of the day - A blog showcasing many Lisp libraries.
• Lisp Koansstars2.8k - The project guides the learner progressively through many Common Lisp language features.
• bknr.datastorestars62 - a CLOS-based lisp-only database in RAM with transaction logging persistence. Manual. licence. (see also chap. 21 of "Common Lisp Recipes")
• cl-migratumstars16 - a system which provides facilities for performing database schema migrations, designed to work with various databases. BSD_3Clause.
• postmodern-passenger-pigeonstars6 - a migration manager for postmodern. No licence specified.
• Programming Algorithms - A comprehensive guide to writing efficient programs with examples in Lisp.
• SBCL's timers, system-wide event schedulers.
• TENstars18 - the completness of Djula meets the usability of Eco. MIT.
• clawkstars34 - an AWK implementation embedded into Common Lisp, to search files for lines and perform specified actions on its fields. BSD-style.
• ScriptLstars62 - Shell scripting made Lisp-like! Or, live-coding remote function calls for the shell. Write a command in the REPL, and run it instantly in the shell. LLGPL.
• 👍 Unix-optsstars78 - a command line options parser with a concise declaration of options. MIT.
• CLONstars43 - a Command Line Options Nuker, with lots of options. ISC.

• replicstars18 - helpers to turn existing code into a readline application, with a focus on defining the completion of the commands' arguments. Also comes as a ready to use executable, that transforms a user's lispy init file into readline commands. MIT.
• cl-ansi-termstars22 - print colorized text, horizontal lines, progress bars, (un)ordered lists and tables on ANSI-compliant terminals. GPL3.
• shclstars246 - a POSIX-like shell in Common Lisp. Apache2.0.
• Shellystars66 - execute Common Lisp functions like a shell command, without the need to write a command line arguments parser. And it also can be used as a Make-like build-tool. FreeBSD.
• Adamsstars137 - UNIX system administration in Common Lisp. Not unlike Ansible, Chef or Puppet. ISC.
• WCLstars62 [staled] - allow hundreds of Lisp
• Buildnodestars16 - A common lisp library to ease interaction with CXML-dom, such as building Excel spreadsheets. BSD.
• CLERICstars34 - a Common Lisp Erlang Interface. An implementation of the Erlang distribution protocol, comparable with erl_interface and jinterface. MIT.
• CodePlayground - an iPhone and iPad app with Lisp support via CCL.
• Vlimestars284 - VLIME: Vim plus Lisp Is Mostly Evil. A Common Lisp dev environment for Vim (and Neovim). MIT.
• Common Lisp chat - Keybase team with well-defined rules and retention policies.
• cl-cffi-gtkstars29 - Binding for GTK+3. GNU LGPL2.1.

• s2i-lispstars25 - Source-to-Image builder image based on CentOS or alternatively RHEL7 for building Common LISP images for OpenShift (and also Docker). It features an up-to-date SBCL with Quicklisp installation, SLIME or SLY integration and allows customization via environment variables. Apache2
• clemstars36 - a matrix library. BSD_2Clause.
• magic-edstars18 - a tiny editing facility, where you can directly load, edit, manipulate and evaluate file or file content from the REPL, when going to a full IDE is too much. MIT.
• cl-isolatedstars23 - A restricted environment for Common Lisp code evaluation AGPL-3.0.
• cl-sentry-clientstars11 - a Sentry client for Common Lisp, the cloud-based error monitoring system. MIT.
• cl-jpegstars17 - Baseline JPEG encoder and decoder library. 3-clause BSD.
• cl-bindgenstars20 - A command line tool and library for creating Common Lisp language bindings from C header files. MIT.
• Software-Evolution-Librarystars92 - The SEL enables the programmatic modification and evaluation of software (C/C++ support using Clang, compiled assembler, and linked ELF binaries). GPL3.
• Linedit - Readline-style library that provides customizable line-editing features. MIT-style.

• gmap - A concise and extensible iteration facility that has the advantage of integrating well with FSet (see the Data Structures section), as it was written by the same author. In Quicklisp as part of misc-extensions. Public domain.
• defstar - a collection of macros for easy inclusion of type declarations for arguments in lambda lists. GNU GPL3
• array-operationsstars35 - a collection of functions and macros for manipulating Common Lisp arrays and performing numerical calculations with them. MIT.
• cl-geometrystars43 - a system for two dimensional computational geometry for Common Lisp. MIT.
• cl-gobject-introspectionstars29 - Gobject Introspection FFI. Automatic bindings to call into the C library. BSD. Generate a lisp interface with gir2clstars5. LGPL3.
• base-lisp-imagestars32 - base Docker image for Common Lisp projects with SBCL or CCL and the latest ASDF, Qlot and Roswell.
• Judge0 IDE is an online editor which supports Common Lisp (SBCL). MIT.

• IUPstars90 - CFFI bindings to the IUP Portable User Interface library (pre-ALPHA). IUP is cross-platform (Windows, macOS, GNU/Linux, with new Android, iOs, Cocoa and Web Assembly drivers), has many widgets, has a small api and is actively developed.
• rutilsstars183 - radical yet reasonable syntactic utilities for Common Lisp. MIT.
• mglstars284 - used by its author to winstars124 the Higgs Boson Machine Learning Challenge. MIT.
• ⭐ triviastars213 - Optimized pattern-matching library. LLGPL.
• cl-yesqlstars34 - SQL statements live in their own files, in SQL syntax, and are imported into Lisp as functions. You are not limited to the features a DSL supports. Based on Clojure's Yesql. MIT.
• 👍 strstars183 - a modern, simple and consistent string manipulation library. MIT.
• trivial-extensible-sequencesstars8 - Portability library for the extensible sequences protocol (SBCL documentation). zlib.

• generic-clstars78 - Generic function interface to standard Common Lisp functions (equality, comparison, arithmetic, objects, iterator, sequences,…). MIT. See also the more lightweight generic-comparabilitystars12. LLGPL.
• cl21stars848 - An experimental project to move Common Lisp into the 21st century. Expat.
• clampstars64 - Arc language's brevity and conciseness to Common Lisp. Artistic License 2.0.
• cl-syntaxstars34 - Reader syntax conventions. LLGPL.
• cl-2dsyntax - An indentation-sensitive reader system. Not available on Quicklisp. Public domain.
• cl-annotstars119 - Python-like annotations for Common Lisp. LLGPL.
• pythonic-string-readerstars13 - A simple and unobtrusive read table modification inspired by Python's three quote strings. BSD_3Clause.
• cl-readerstars6 - A utility library
• ppathstars26 - Common Lisp's
• git-apistars14 - Common Lisp library
• jonathanstars125 - A JSON encoder and decoder. MIT.
• Planet Lisp - A meta blog that collects the contents of various Lisp-related blogs.
• filtered-functionsstars33 - enable the use of arbitrary predicates for selecting and applying methods. MIT.

• cl-prevalence - in-memory database system. Implementation of Object Prevalence, in which business objects are kept live in memory and transactions are journaled for system recovery. github forkstars16. LLGPL. See also cl-prevalence-multimasterstars2, to syncronize multiple cl-prevalence systems state.
• pngload-fast - A PNG (Portable Network Graphics) image format decoder in portable Common Lisp with an emphasis on speed. MIT.
• fast-httpstars323 - A fast HTTP request/response parser for Common Lisp. MIT.
• zaservestars6 - A portable fork of AllegroServe, by Franz Inc. LLGPL.
• cl-http2-protocolstars102 - a pure Common Lisp transport agnostic implementation of the HTTP/2 protocol at draft-14. MIT.
• hunchentoot-cgistars4 - a library for executing CGI scripts from the hunchentoot webserver. BSD.
• hunchentoot-multi-acceptorstars1 - Route multiple domains (virtual hosts) on a single hunchentoot acceptor using a single port. Apache2.0.
• avmstars38 - Efficient and expressive arrayed vector math library with multi-threading and CUDA support. MIT.
• cl-coroutinestars47 - a coroutine library. It uses the CL-CONT continuations library in its implementation. MIT.
• erlangenstars62 - Distributed, asynchronous message passing system for Clozure Common Lisp. GNU GPL3.
• Actorsstars8 package for LispWorks (announce) MIT.
• common-lisp-actorsstars94 - a simple and easy to use Actor system. BSD_2Clause.

• memento-moristars26- a library for writing robust, actor-based systems. It draws inspiration from Erlang/OTP, particularly its crash-first approach to high-availability systems, as well as Akka. MIT.
• remote-jsstars29 - send JavaScript from Common Lisp to a browser. MIT.
• dh-quicklisp-buildappstars7 - debhelper utility to let you compile your quicklisp-based Common Lisp code into a buildapp binary in a .deb with almost no effort. MIT.
• cl-hooksstars13 - Hooks extension point mechanism (as known, e.g., from GNU Emacs). LGPL.
• method-hooks - When CLOS method combination allow only one hook per method, this library allows an arbitrary number of them. Mozilla Public Licence.
• cl-algebraic-data-typestars86 - A library for defining algebraic data types in a similar spirit to Haskell or ML. 3-clause BSD.
• 👍 trivial-typesstars56 - provides missing but important type definitions such as proper-list, association-list, property-list and tuple. LLGPL.
• chameleonstars8 - a configuration management library shipped with profile support. MIT.
• inlined-generic-functionstars107 -

• a-cl-loggerstars12 - Logging library providing context sensitive logging of more than just strings to more than just local files or output streams. Features logstash support, json support, logger hierarchies, context sensitive logging, objects printed as an inspectable presentation,…
• linear-programming – a library for solving linear programming problems. MIT.
• trivial-utf8 - A small library for doing UTF-8-based I/O. BSD.
• Common Lisp Standard Draft (pdf) - The standard draft of the Common Lisp specifications, in a well formatted PDF with a sidebar.
• swank-crewstars33 - distributed computation framework implemented using Swank Client. BSD_3Clause.
• cl-punchstars14 - Scala-like anonymous lambda literals. (mapcar ^(* 2 _) '(1 2 3 4 5)). MIT.
• sanity-clausestars28 - a data serialization/contract library for Common Lisp. Schemas can be property lists or class-based, allowing to check slots' types during make-instance. LLGPL.
• specialization-storestars28 - generic functions based on types. Simplified BSD License variant.
• Lisp Discord Server
• Practical Common Lisp - A good introductory text to Common Lisp, with practical examples. Better read with a Firefox add-onstars10.
• Bikestars85 - a cross-platform .Net Core interface. MIT.
• Foil - A foreign object interface; works with the JVM and CLI. Not available on Quicklisp. CPL 1.0.
• babel2stars2 - A Fluid Construction Grammar implementation, computational framework, and unification-based grammar formalism Apache2.0.
• repl-utilitiesstars39 - Ease
• mgl-paxstars38 - Exploratory
• cl4pystars54 - The library cl4py (pronounce as clappy) allows Python programs to call Common Lisp libraries. MIT.
• py4clstars119 - A library that allows Common Lisp code to access Python libraries. It is basically the inverse of cl4py. MIT.
• cl-pythonstars317 - an implementation of Python in Common Lisp. LLGPL.
• cl-gearmanstars19 - a library for the Gearman distributed job system. LLGPL.
• cl-colliderstars144 - A

• cl-anastars162 - Common Lisp data analysis library with emphasis on modularity and conceptual clarity. It aims to be a general purpose framework for analyzing small and large scale datasets, including binned data analysis and visualization. GNU GPL3.
• numclstars575 - Numpy clone in Common Lisp. LGPL3.
